response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals and 15+ years of development experience, there is a severe mismatch on core mandatory requirements. Professional C++ experience totals approximately 6 months at National Instruments (2014-2015), far short of the required 5-10 years. No evidence of SQL Server or Oracle expertise. Most critically, the resume shows zero exposure to capital markets, fixed income, investment banking, or financial services domains—both of which are explicitly mandatory. The candidate's career trajectory over the past decade has been web/full-stack (Node.js, TypeScript, D Lang), moving away from systems languages and enterprise finance.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship practices including TDD, CI/CD pipelines, and infrastructure automation across multiple roles
Demonstrated ability to quickly acquire new technologies and domains, with experience across web, mobile, desktop, embedded, and GIS platforms
Excellent communication and teaching skills, evidenced by workshops, talks, and open-source project leadership
concerns: Only ~6 months of professional C++ experience (2014-2015); current focus is TypeScript, Node.js, and D Lang—moving away from systems languages
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; only mentions generic SQL and MongoDB; mandatory SQL Server/Oracle expertise is absent
Zero capital markets, fixed income, investment banking, or financial services domain experience—both alternatives for mandatory domain requirement are unmet
